project.name: decision-simulation-engine
project.description: A production-ready MVP for interactive branching scenarios (decision-based simulations) powered by a JSON-defined state machine, pairing a Next.js learner UI with a FastAPI execution API, a pure Python engine and safe expression parser, and a Postgres event/reflection store with analytics and CSV export. Note: this project has over 100 source files; consider scoping analysis to a subdirectory for faster results.

nodes.name: admin.py
nodes.summary: Admin FastAPI router (prefix /admin) guarded by an X-Admin-Key dependency, exposing scenario import, version creation/publishing, analytics, and CSV export endpoints.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses FastAPI Depends() for router-level auth and engine validator integration before persistence.

nodes.name: import_scenario
nodes.summary: POST /scenarios/import handler that validates scenario JSON via the engine validator, then creates the scenario record plus version 1.

nodes.name: get_scenario
nodes.summary: GET /scenarios/{scenario_id} handler returning a scenario together with all of its versions.

nodes.name: create_version
nodes.summary: POST /scenarios/{scenario_id}/versions handler that validates and persists an additional auto-numbered scenario version.

nodes.name: publish_version
nodes.summary: Publish handler that transitions a version to published status while archiving any previously published version of the same scenario.

nodes.name: scenario_analytics
nodes.summary: GET analytics handler that delegates to the analytics service to return aggregated play metrics, optionally scoped to a version number.

nodes.name: scenario_export_csv
nodes.summary: CSV export handler that delegates to the export service and returns play data as a downloadable CSV response.

nodes.name: play.py
nodes.summary: SQLAlchemy ORM models for play sessions, event-sourced events, and reflections, with events acting as the source of truth for engine state replay.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Postgres JSONB, native enums, and Mapped/mapped_column typed ORM with cascade deletes flowing through Play.

nodes.name: EventType
nodes.summary: String enum enumerating all event types recorded during a play session (start, view_scene, choose, auto_advance, conditional_advance, go_back, complete).

nodes.name: Play
nodes.summary: ORM model for a single learner's play session, pinned to one immutable scenario version with relationships to its events and reflection.

nodes.name: Event
nodes.summary: ORM model for an event-sourced row carrying sequence number, event type, scene transitions, and a delta_json of variable effects.

nodes.name: Reflection
nodes.summary: ORM model for a learner's post-completion reflection, stored 1-to-1 with a Play via a unique foreign key.

nodes.name: scenario.py
nodes.summary: SQLAlchemy ORM models for scenarios and their versioned JSONB snapshots, with a status enum and per-scenario monotonic version numbering.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses UUID primary keys, JSONB scenario documents, and a native Postgres enum for version status.

nodes.name: VersionStatus
nodes.summary: String enum defining the lifecycle states of a scenario version: draft, published, and archived.

nodes.name: Scenario
nodes.summary: ORM model for a scenario identified by a unique slug, with metadata timestamps and an ordered relationship to its versions.

nodes.name: ScenarioVersion
nodes.summary: ORM model for a versioned snapshot of a scenario, storing the scenario_json JSONB document, status, and a per-scenario unique version number.

nodes.name: play_repo.py
nodes.summary: Data-access repository for play sessions, event sourcing, and reflections, assigning monotonic event sequence numbers and supporting go-back truncation and engine replay.
nodes.languageNotes: Caller-owned transactions; methods flush() but never commit(), with selectinload for eager relationship loading.

nodes.name: PlayRepository
nodes.summary: Repository class wrapping a SQLAlchemy session that creates plays, appends and queries sequenced events, truncates events for go-back, and records reflections.

nodes.name: scenario_repo.py
nodes.summary: Data-access repository for scenarios and versions, handling per-scenario monotonic version numbering and publish/archive lifecycle transitions.
nodes.languageNotes: Dependency-injected session with flush-only writes; assigns version numbers via func.max()+1 within the transaction.

nodes.name: ScenarioRepository
nodes.summary: Repository class wrapping a SQLAlchemy session that creates scenarios and versions, looks up published versions by slug, and publishes or archives versions.

nodes.name: admin.py
nodes.summary: Pydantic request and response schemas for the admin API, covering scenario/version output DTOs and import, version-create, and publish payloads.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses ConfigDict(from_attributes=True) so ORM objects (and UUIDs) serialize directly.

nodes.name: analytics.py
nodes.summary: Analytics aggregation service producing admin reporting dicts from event and play data, with optional filtering to a single scenario version.

nodes.name: _resolve_version_ids
nodes.summary: Helper resolving the UUIDs of matching ScenarioVersion rows for a scenario, optionally narrowed to one version number.

nodes.name: get_analytics
nodes.summary: Computes aggregated play analytics (counts, completion, choice and reflection metrics) for a scenario, optionally scoped to a version number.

nodes.name: export.py
nodes.summary: CSV export service producing one row per play with dynamic reflection_N columns and a derived scene-visit path string.

nodes.name: export_csv
nodes.summary: Builds a UTF-8 CSV string for all plays of a scenario, including the visited scene path and per-question reflection answer columns.

nodes.name: test_models_play.py
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the Play, Event, and Reflection ORM models, verifying persistence, defaults, and constraints via factory helpers.

nodes.name: test_models_scenario.py
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the Scenario and ScenarioVersion ORM models, covering field persistence, defaults, and version relationships.

nodes.name: test_play_repo.py
nodes.summary: Unit tests for PlayRepository covering play creation, event append/sequencing, go-back truncation, engine replay formatting, and reflections.

nodes.name: test_public_play_back.py
nodes.summary: Integration tests for the public go-back endpoint, asserting state rewind behavior across choices, auto-advance, completion, and repeated backs.

nodes.name: test_public_play_reflection.py
nodes.summary: Integration tests for the public reflection-submission endpoint, covering validation errors and successful reflection persistence.

nodes.name: test_public_play_start.py
nodes.summary: Integration tests for the public start-play endpoint, verifying status, initial scene, progress, learner label, and emitted start events.

nodes.name: test_public_play_step.py
nodes.summary: Integration tests for the public step endpoint across choice, auto-advance, conditional, and completion scene transitions plus error cases.

nodes.name: test_public_play_view.py
nodes.summary: Integration tests for the public get-play view endpoint, asserting derived play state after start, step, truncate, completion, and reflection.

nodes.name: test_scenario_repo.py
nodes.summary: Unit tests for ScenarioRepository covering scenario creation, slug lookup, version numbering, and publish/archive lifecycle transitions.

nodes.name: page.test.tsx
nodes.summary: Jest/React Testing Library tests for the play-completion page, verifying outcome rendering, reflection form submission, and redirect behavior.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Next.js client page that displays the completed play outcome and renders the reflection form, redirecting back to play if the session is not yet done.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Next.js scenario landing page that fetches scenario metadata by slug and starts a new play session before routing the learner into the play flow.

nodes.name: page.test.tsx
nodes.summary: Jest/React Testing Library tests for the play page, covering scene rendering, stepping forward, going back, and completion redirects.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Next.js client page driving the interactive play loop: fetches current play state, dispatches step/back mutations, renders scenes, and redirects to the completion page when done.

nodes.name: ReflectionForm.tsx
nodes.summary: Controlled reflection form component that collects learner name and per-question responses, validates input, and submits the reflection via the API client.

nodes.name: SceneRenderer.test.tsx
nodes.summary: Jest/React Testing Library tests for SceneRenderer, asserting the correct scene subcomponent is rendered for each scene type.

nodes.name: SceneRenderer.tsx
nodes.summary: Dispatcher component that selects and renders the appropriate scene component (choice, continue, or end) based on the scene type.

nodes.name: ChoiceScene.tsx
nodes.summary: Scene component rendering a choice prompt with selectable options that invoke the onChoose callback with the chosen index.

nodes.name: ContinueScene.tsx
nodes.summary: Scene component rendering narration with a continue button that advances auto-advance and conditional scenes.

nodes.name: EndScene.tsx
nodes.summary: Scene component rendering the terminal outcome and outcome message that concludes a play session.

nodes.name: client.ts
nodes.summary: Typed fetch-based API client for the public Branching Scenarios endpoints, wrapping scenario, play, step, back, and reflection calls and throwing ApiClientError on non-2xx responses.
nodes.languageNotes: Generic apiFetch<T> wrapper centralizes URL building, JSON handling, and error normalization.

nodes.name: types.ts
nodes.summary: TypeScript interface definitions mirroring the FastAPI public Pydantic schemas, describing scenario, scene, play, step, back, and reflection request/response shapes.

nodes.name: CompletePage
nodes.summary: React page component that loads the completed play, displays its outcome, and embeds the reflection form, redirecting to play when not yet done.

nodes.name: ScenarioPage
nodes.summary: React page component that fetches scenario metadata by slug and starts a new play via a mutation, routing the learner into the play page.

nodes.name: PlayPage
nodes.summary: React page component orchestrating the play loop with step and back mutations, optimistic cache updates, scene rendering, and completion redirects.

nodes.name: ReflectionForm
nodes.summary: Controlled form component managing learner name and per-question response state, performing client-side validation and submitting the reflection.

nodes.name: SceneRenderer
nodes.summary: Switches on scene type to render the matching ChoiceScene, ContinueScene, or EndScene component.

nodes.name: ChoiceScene
nodes.summary: Renders a choice scene's options as buttons that call onChoose with the selected choice index.

nodes.name: ContinueScene
nodes.summary: Renders narration with a continue button that triggers onContinue to advance the play.

nodes.name: EndScene
nodes.summary: Renders the terminal scene outcome and message that concludes a play session.

nodes.name: ApiClientError
nodes.summary: Error subclass carrying the HTTP status code alongside a human-readable message for failed API calls.

nodes.name: apiFetch
nodes.summary: Generic fetch wrapper that builds the public API URL, sets JSON headers, parses responses, and throws ApiClientError on non-2xx status.

nodes.name: getScenario
nodes.summary: Fetches public metadata for the latest published version of a scenario by slug.

nodes.name: startPlay
nodes.summary: Creates a new play session for a scenario version and returns the play ID and initial scene.

nodes.name: getPlay
nodes.summary: Reconstructs and returns the full current state of a play session by ID.

nodes.name: stepPlay
nodes.summary: Advances a play by one step, optionally passing a choice index for choice scenes.

nodes.name: backPlay
nodes.summary: Undoes the last step of a play and returns the previous scene.

nodes.name: submitReflection
nodes.summary: Submits a learner reflection for a completed play session.

nodes.name: deps.py
nodes.summary: FastAPI dependency module providing admin API-key verification used to guard administrative routes.

nodes.name: verify_admin_key
nodes.summary: Reads the admin API key from request headers and constant-time compares it against the configured key, raising 403 on mismatch.

nodes.name: public.py
nodes.summary: Learner-facing v1 API router driving scenario playthroughs: start/step/back navigation, play views, and reflection submission backed by the event-sourced engine.
nodes.languageNotes: Rebuilds engine state by replaying persisted events (event sourcing) on each navigation request.

nodes.name: _compute_progress
nodes.summary: Derives step count and choices-made totals from a play's event list to build a ProgressOut DTO.

nodes.name: _build_scene_dto
nodes.summary: Maps a raw engine scene dict into the learner-facing SceneDTO, normalizing choices, narration, and media URLs.

nodes.name: compute_play_view
nodes.summary: Reconstructs the current play state by replaying stored events through the engine and assembles the full PlayViewResponse.

nodes.name: get_scenario
nodes.summary: Public endpoint returning the published version of a scenario by slug, exposing metadata and reflection configuration.

nodes.name: start_play
nodes.summary: Creates a new play row, runs the engine start, persists the initial scene-entered event, and returns the first scene.

nodes.name: get_play
nodes.summary: Public endpoint returning the current view of an in-progress play by delegating to compute_play_view.

nodes.name: step_play
nodes.summary: Advances a play by applying a learner choice through the engine, persisting choice/scene/completion events and returning the next scene or outcome.

nodes.name: back_play
nodes.summary: Implements go-back navigation by truncating events after the previous scene and recomputing engine state to the prior step.

nodes.name: submit_reflection
nodes.summary: Validates a completed play and persists the learner's reflection responses, rejecting duplicates and incomplete plays.

nodes.name: config.py
nodes.summary: Application settings module defining environment-driven configuration (database URL, media base, admin key, CORS origins).

nodes.name: Settings
nodes.summary: Pydantic settings object loading runtime configuration from environment variables and parsing the CORS origins list.

nodes.name: session.py
nodes.summary: Database session module configuring the SQLAlchemy engine/session factory and the get_db request dependency.

nodes.name: get_db
nodes.summary: FastAPI dependency yielding a scoped SQLAlchemy session and ensuring it is closed after the request.

nodes.name: main.py
nodes.summary: FastAPI application entry point that wires CORS, mounts the admin and public v1 routers, and exposes a health check.

nodes.name: health
nodes.summary: Lightweight liveness endpoint returning an ok status payload.

nodes.name: public.py
nodes.summary: Pydantic request/response schemas for the learner-facing public API, covering scenes, progress, play lifecycle, and reflections.

nodes.name: ChoiceOut
nodes.summary: Output schema representing a single selectable choice's display text.

nodes.name: SceneDTO
nodes.summary: Learner-facing scene payload including type, narration, media, choices, and end outcome fields.

nodes.name: ProgressOut
nodes.summary: Progress payload reporting step count and choices made within a play.

nodes.name: ScenarioMetadataOut
nodes.summary: Public scenario metadata schema exposing title, description, page branding, author, and completion tracking flags.

nodes.name: ScenarioPublicResponse
nodes.summary: Response for fetching a published scenario, bundling slug, version, metadata, start scene, and reflection prompts.

nodes.name: PlayStartRequest
nodes.summary: Request schema to begin a play, carrying the target scenario version id and optional learner label.

nodes.name: PlayStartResponse
nodes.summary: Response returned when a play starts, containing play id, version id, the first scene, and initial progress.

nodes.name: StepRequest
nodes.summary: Request schema for advancing a play, carrying the selected choice index.

nodes.name: StepResponse
nodes.summary: Response after stepping a play, reporting the next scene, progress, done flag, and outcome details.

nodes.name: BackResponse
nodes.summary: Response for go-back navigation, returning the recomputed prior scene, progress, and done flag.

nodes.name: ReflectionRequest
nodes.summary: Request schema for submitting reflection answers, carrying a responses map and optional student name.

nodes.name: ReflectionResponse
nodes.summary: Minimal acknowledgement schema returned after a reflection is stored.

nodes.name: PlayViewResponse
nodes.summary: Full play view payload including current scene, progress, completion state, outcome, and reflection requirements.

nodes.name: test_admin_analytics.py
nodes.summary: Integration tests for admin analytics aggregation and CSV export endpoints, including version filtering and content escaping.

nodes.name: _import_scenario
nodes.summary: Test helper that imports a scenario via the admin API and returns the parsed response.

nodes.name: populated_scenario
nodes.summary: Fixture that seeds a scenario with multiple plays, steps, and reflections to exercise analytics aggregation.

nodes.name: TestAnalytics404
nodes.summary: Tests that the analytics endpoint returns 404 for unknown scenarios and enforces the admin key.

nodes.name: TestAnalyticsEmpty
nodes.summary: Tests that analytics for a scenario with no plays returns 200 with zeroed counts.

nodes.name: TestAnalyticsData
nodes.summary: Tests computed analytics metrics: total/completed plays, completion rate, drop-off, choice distribution, and reflections.

nodes.name: TestAnalyticsVersionFilter
nodes.summary: Tests analytics version-number filtering across multiple scenario versions and aggregation behavior.

nodes.name: TestExportBasic
nodes.summary: Tests the CSV export endpoint's auth, content type, content-disposition header, and empty-scenario behavior.

nodes.name: TestExportColumns
nodes.summary: Tests that exported CSV includes the expected standard and reflection columns with the correct row count.

nodes.name: TestExportContent
nodes.summary: Tests CSV row content: completion flag, outcome, scene path formatting, learner label, and reflection escaping.

nodes.name: TestExportVersionFilter
nodes.summary: Tests CSV export filtering by version number, validating row counts and per-version content.

nodes.name: test_admin_auth.py
nodes.summary: Tests for the admin API-key dependency and the admin router's OpenAPI tagging/auth wiring.

nodes.name: TestVerifyAdminKey
nodes.summary: Tests verify_admin_key behavior for valid, missing, wrong, empty, and case-sensitive keys including the 403 detail message.

nodes.name: TestAdminRouterAuth
nodes.summary: Verifies the admin router is tagged in the generated OpenAPI schema.

nodes.name: test_admin_endpoints.py
nodes.summary: Integration tests for admin scenario import, retrieval, version creation, publishing lifecycle, and the health endpoint.

nodes.name: import_scenario
nodes.summary: Test helper that posts a scenario import request with admin headers and a configurable status.

nodes.name: TestImportScenario
nodes.summary: Tests scenario import: 201 responses, returned ids, validation errors, duplicate slug conflicts, and auth.

nodes.name: TestGetScenario
nodes.summary: Tests admin scenario retrieval including version listing, unknown id 404, and admin-key enforcement.

nodes.name: TestCreateVersion
nodes.summary: Tests creating new scenario versions, version-number incrementing, validation errors, and unknown-scenario 404.

nodes.name: TestPublishVersion
nodes.summary: Tests the publish lifecycle, ensuring the published status is set and previously published versions are archived.

nodes.name: TestHealth
nodes.summary: Verifies the health endpoint returns an ok status.

nodes.name: test_public_scenario.py
nodes.summary: Integration tests for the public scenario-by-slug endpoint covering metadata, reflection fields, version selection, and 404 cases.

nodes.name: TestGetPublicScenario
nodes.summary: Tests fetching published scenarios by slug, including metadata, reflection prompts, highest-version selection, draft exclusion, and no-auth access.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package entry point for the engine package; re-exports ScenarioEngine and validate_scenario as the public API and sets __version__.
nodes.languageNotes: Python package barrel using `import ... as ...` re-export aliasing with noqa F401 to expose a curated public surface.

nodes.name: engine.py
nodes.summary: Defines ScenarioEngine, the single stateless public entry point the FastAPI service uses to start, step through, and rewind branching scenario play sessions.
nodes.languageNotes: Stateless design: every method receives or returns an EngineState so the API layer owns persistence; dispatch uses isinstance checks on the discriminated scene union.

nodes.name: ScenarioEngine
nodes.summary: Validates and loads a scenario, then drives play via start(), step() (dispatches to the per-type executor for the current scene), and rewind() (replays a truncated event list to reconstruct prior state).

nodes.name: executors.py
nodes.summary: One pure executor function per scene type (choice, auto-advance, conditional, end); each works on a defensive copy of EngineState and returns an ExecutorResult describing the transition.
nodes.languageNotes: execute_conditional uses dependency injection for its evaluate_fn (defaulting to expr.safe_evaluate via a lazy import), enabling test fixtures to swap in simple lambdas.

nodes.name: ExecutorResult
nodes.summary: NamedTuple return type shared by all executor functions, carrying the new state, a done flag, and optional outcome/outcome_message for end scenes.

nodes.name: _copy_state
nodes.summary: Returns a shallow-but-safe copy of an EngineState (copying the variables dict and history list) so executors never mutate the caller's state, avoiding deepcopy overhead.

nodes.name: execute_choice
nodes.summary: Advances from a choice scene by applying the selected choice's variable effects, recording a post-effect history entry, and transitioning to the chosen next scene.

nodes.name: execute_auto_advance
nodes.summary: Advances from an auto-advance scene without player input, recording a history entry and transitioning to the scene's fixed next target.

nodes.name: execute_conditional
nodes.summary: Advances from a conditional scene by evaluating branch expressions in order (via an injectable evaluate_fn), falling back to the scene default, and transitioning to the first matching target.

nodes.name: execute_end
nodes.summary: Handles an end scene by copying the state unchanged and returning done=True along with the scene's outcome and outcome_message to signal session completion.

nodes.name: models.py
nodes.summary: Pydantic v2 models defining the scenario JSON contract: the Scene discriminated union (choice/auto_advance/conditional/end), Choice/Condition leaves, ScenarioMetadata, and the top-level Scenario.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a Pydantic Annotated discriminated union keyed on the `type` literal field and extra="ignore" config for forward-compatibility with unknown JSON fields.

nodes.name: Choice
nodes.summary: One selectable option inside a choice scene, with display text, a next scene id, and a dict of numeric variable effects.

nodes.name: Condition
nodes.summary: A single branch inside a conditional scene, pairing an expression string (evaluated by expr.safe_evaluate) with the next scene id.

nodes.name: SceneBase
nodes.summary: Base model holding fields common to every scene type: title, optional image, description, and narration.

nodes.name: ChoiceScene
nodes.summary: Scene subtype (type="choice") presenting a list of Choice options for the player to select from.

nodes.name: AutoAdvanceScene
nodes.summary: Scene subtype (type="auto_advance") that transitions automatically to a fixed next scene with no player input.

nodes.name: ConditionalScene
nodes.summary: Scene subtype (type="conditional") that branches based on an ordered list of Conditions, with an optional default fallback scene id.

nodes.name: EndScene
nodes.summary: Terminal scene subtype (type="end") carrying an optional outcome and outcome_message that mark the play session complete.

nodes.name: ScenarioMetadata
nodes.summary: Descriptive metadata for a scenario such as title, description, page title/icon, author, version, completion tracking flag, and cover image.

nodes.name: Scenario
nodes.summary: Top-level scenario model bundling metadata, reflection questions/prompts, initial variables, the start scene id, and the keyed map of Scene objects.

nodes.name: state.py
nodes.summary: Defines the mutable play-session working state: HistoryEntry (frozen transition record) and EngineState (current scene, variables, history) with JSON to_dict/from_dict serialisation for the event-sourcing API.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Python dataclasses — frozen=True for the immutable HistoryEntry and a mutable EngineState with field(default_factory=...) defaults.

nodes.name: HistoryEntry
nodes.summary: Frozen dataclass recording one scene-to-next-scene transition, with optional choice index/text and a diagnostic variables snapshot; to_dict() emits a compact JSON-safe shape omitting None fields.

nodes.name: EngineState
nodes.summary: Mutable session state holding the current scene id, variables, and transition history; apply_effects() mutates variables in place and to_dict/from_dict round-trip the state for persistence.

nodes.name: validator.py
nodes.summary: Provides validate_scenario, which performs Pydantic structural validation followed by semantic referential-integrity and expression-syntax checks, collecting all errors into a list.

nodes.name: validate_scenario
nodes.summary: Validates a scenario JSON in two phases — Pydantic structural validation then semantic checks (start/next references exist, conditional expressions parse, defaults resolve) — returning all human-readable errors.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Public package entry point for the safe-expression library, re-exporting parse and safe_evaluate as the supported API surface.
nodes.languageNotes: Python package barrel using explicit re-export aliasing (import x as x) to satisfy linters and mark public re-exports.

nodes.name: ast_nodes.py
nodes.summary: Defines the immutable AST node types (Literal, Variable, UnaryOp, BinaryOp) and the ExprNode union that the parser builds and the evaluator walks.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses frozen dataclasses for immutability and a Union type alias declared after the classes so all member names are in scope.

nodes.name: evaluator.py
nodes.summary: Recursively evaluates the expression AST and exposes safe_evaluate, the fail-closed parse-and-evaluate entry point used by the engine's conditional scenes (no eval() anywhere).
nodes.languageNotes: Switches on TokenType enum values shared with the lexer; fail-closed contract catches all exceptions and returns False.

nodes.name: lexer.py
nodes.summary: Hand-written tokenizer that scans an expression string into a token stream, recognizing identifiers, booleans, numbers (including negatives/floats), comparison/logical operators, and parentheses.
nodes.languageNotes: Single-pass character scanner with one-character lookahead (_peek) and explicit position tracking; raises LexError on unexpected characters.

nodes.name: parser.py
nodes.summary: Recursive-descent parser that turns a token stream into an AST honoring operator precedence (or/and/equality/comparison/unary/primary), plus a module-level parse() that lexes then parses.
nodes.languageNotes: Classic precedence-climbing recursive descent; each grammar rule is its own method and parentheses recurse back into the top-level rule.

nodes.name: tokens.py
nodes.summary: Declares the TokenType enum (operators, values, grouping, EOF sentinel) and the immutable Token dataclass shared across the lexer, parser, and evaluator.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses enum.auto() for token kinds and a frozen dataclass with an optional value payload.

nodes.name: Literal
nodes.summary: Immutable AST leaf holding a numeric or boolean constant value.

nodes.name: Variable
nodes.summary: Immutable AST leaf referencing a named scenario variable resolved at evaluation time.

nodes.name: UnaryOp
nodes.summary: Immutable AST node for a unary operation (currently logical NOT) wrapping a single operand.

nodes.name: BinaryOp
nodes.summary: Immutable AST node for a binary operation (comparison or logical) carrying left/right subtrees and the operator TokenType.

nodes.name: EvaluationError
nodes.summary: Exception raised when the evaluator encounters an unsupported operator or unknown AST node type.

nodes.name: evaluate
nodes.summary: Recursively evaluates an AST node against a variable map, dispatching on node type and TokenType to compute literals, variable lookups, NOT, logical, and comparison results.

nodes.name: safe_evaluate
nodes.summary: Primary public API that parses and evaluates an expression string, returning False on any lex/parse/lookup/type error (fail-closed).

nodes.name: LexError
nodes.summary: Exception raised by the lexer for unexpected or malformed characters in the input string.

nodes.name: Lexer
nodes.summary: Stateful single-pass tokenizer that scans the input string character by character and produces Token objects via next_token / tokenize.

nodes.name: ParseError
nodes.summary: Exception raised by the parser for empty input, unexpected tokens, or unbalanced parentheses.

nodes.name: Parser
nodes.summary: Recursive-descent parser whose per-grammar-rule methods consume a token list and build a precedence-correct AST of ExprNode instances.

nodes.name: parse
nodes.summary: Module-level convenience function that lexes an expression string and parses it into the AST root node.

nodes.name: TokenType
nodes.summary: Enum enumerating every token kind (logical/comparison operators, grouping, identifier, number, boolean, and EOF sentinel) shared across lexer, parser, and evaluator.

nodes.name: Token
nodes.summary: Immutable dataclass pairing a TokenType with an optional literal value (number, boolean, or identifier text).

nodes.name: env.py
nodes.summary: Alembic migration environment that wires Base.metadata to the migration context and reads DATABASE_URL from app settings, supporting both offline (SQL emission) and online (live connection) migration modes.
nodes.languageNotes: Performs a side-effect import of app.models so all ORM classes register on Base.metadata before autogeneration.

nodes.name: run_migrations_offline
nodes.summary: Runs Alembic migrations in offline mode, configuring the context with literal binds to emit SQL without a live database connection.

nodes.name: run_migrations_online
nodes.summary: Runs Alembic migrations in online mode by creating an engine with a NullPool and applying migrations against a live database connection.

nodes.name: base.py
nodes.summary: Defines the project-wide SQLAlchemy declarative Base whose metadata aggregates every ORM table for migrations and table creation.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses SQLAlchemy 2.0 DeclarativeBase as the single shared declarative root.

nodes.name: Base
nodes.summary: Project-wide SQLAlchemy declarative base class that all ORM models inherit from so their tables are collected in a single metadata registry.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Models package barrel that re-exports the Scenario and Play ORM classes and enums, ensuring all model metadata is registered on Base for migrations and tests.

nodes.name: conftest.py
nodes.summary: Shared pytest fixtures for API integration tests, providing a session-scoped engine that creates tables and a per-test transactional Session that rolls back via savepoints for isolation.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses join_transaction_mode="create_savepoint" so deliberate IntegrityErrors only roll back a savepoint, keeping the outer transaction valid.

nodes.name: db_engine
nodes.summary: Session-scoped pytest fixture that creates the SQLAlchemy engine, skips DB tests if the database is unreachable, and idempotently creates all tables.

nodes.name: db
nodes.summary: Per-test pytest fixture yielding a Session bound to a connection whose work is rolled back after the test for clean isolation between cases.

nodes.name: Dockerfile
nodes.summary: Multi-stage Docker build for the Next.js web app: installs dependencies, compiles the standalone bundle, and runs a minimal hardened production image as a non-root user on port 3000.
nodes.languageNotes: Three-stage build (deps -> builder -> runner) leverages Next.js standalone output to shrink the final image and runs as a non-root nextjs user (uid 1001).

nodes.name: deps
nodes.summary: Dependency-install stage that copies package manifests and runs a clean `npm ci` for reproducible node_modules.

nodes.name: builder
nodes.summary: Build stage that compiles the Next.js app, baking the NEXT_PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL into the client bundle and producing the standalone server output.

nodes.name: runner
nodes.summary: Minimal production runtime stage that copies the standalone build and serves it as a non-root user, exposing port 3000.

nodes.name: .dockerignore
nodes.summary: Defines Docker build-context exclusions for the web app, omitting node_modules, build artifacts, env files, docs, and test output to keep the image small and builds reproducible.

nodes.name: Dockerfile
nodes.summary: Single-stage Docker build for the FastAPI service on python:3.12-slim that installs the local engine and expr monorepo packages plus API requirements, then runs the app via uvicorn on port 8000.
nodes.languageNotes: Build context must be the repo root so the COPY of packages/expr and packages/engine resolves; docker-compose overrides CMD to prepend an alembic migration step.

nodes.name: .dockerignore
nodes.summary: Docker build-context exclusion list for the API service that omits Python caches, virtualenvs, VCS metadata, tests, docs, and coverage artifacts to keep the image lean.

nodes.name: .dockerignore
nodes.summary: Root-level Docker build-context exclusion list used when docker-compose builds the api service from the repo root, dropping git metadata, frontend node_modules/.next, Python bytecode, test artefacts, and local secrets from the image.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Top-level project documentation for the Branching Scenarios MVP, covering quickstart, architecture, repo layout, the scenario JSON contract, the safe expression grammar, the /v1 API contract, the pure-Python engine surface, local dev, and env vars.
nodes.languageNotes: Spec-style README documenting both the runtime API contract and the author-facing JSON/expression contracts across 10 numbered sections.

nodes.name: SAMPLE_SCENARIO.json
nodes.summary: Minimal 3-scene reference scenario that documents the JSON import contract (slug/title/description plus a scenario_json with metadata, reflection prompts, variables, start_scene_id, and choice/auto_advance/end scenes) consumed by the admin import endpoint.
nodes.languageNotes: Serves as the canonical example of the scene-keyed state-machine format (choice, auto_advance, end) referenced throughout the README and engine.

nodes.name: .env.local.example
nodes.summary: Example environment file for the Next.js web app, documenting the NEXT_PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL setting that points the browser API client at the FastAPI backend.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: npm manifest for the Next.js web app, declaring React/Next/React-Query dependencies and dev/build/test scripts (next dev, next build, jest, playwright).
nodes.languageNotes: Defines both Jest unit tests and Playwright e2e test scripts alongside the Next.js dev/build/start lifecycle.

nodes.name: tsconfig.json
nodes.summary: TypeScript compiler configuration for the Next.js web app enabling strict mode, bundler module resolution, the Next plugin, and the '@/*' path alias.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses moduleResolution 'bundler' with the Next.js TS plugin and a '@/*' path alias mapping to the project root.

nodes.name: ADR-001-stack.md
nodes.summary: Architecture Decision Record accepting the Next.js + FastAPI + Postgres stack, citing modern SSR routing on the frontend, an async-friendly Python backend, and a relational/JSONB analytics-friendly database.

nodes.name: ADR-002-event-sourcing-go-back.md
nodes.summary: Architecture Decision Record adopting immutable per-play events with deterministic replay to power the rewind/go-back feature, motivated by auditability, mutation-free rewind, and path analytics.

nodes.name: ADR-003-versioning.md
nodes.summary: Architecture Decision Record introducing a scenario_versions table in the MVP to support draft-versus-published states, rollback, and protection of active learners from breaking changes.

nodes.name: ADR-004-safe-expressions.md
nodes.summary: Architecture Decision Record mandating a custom AST-based expression parser instead of eval, chosen for security, deterministic behavior, and a stable author contract.

nodes.name: alembic.ini
nodes.summary: Alembic configuration declaring the migration script location, file naming template, and logging setup; the sqlalchemy.url placeholder is overridden at runtime by env.py using settings.DATABASE_URL so credentials stay out of the file.
nodes.languageNotes: INI format with separate [alembic], [post_write_hooks], and Python logging sections (loggers/handlers/formatters).

nodes.name: pytest.ini
nodes.summary: Pytest configuration for the API service enabling strict asyncio mode and a function-scoped default event loop for async test fixtures.

nodes.name: requirements.txt

nodes.name: 20260219_0001_initial_schema.py

nodes.name: scenarios

nodes.name: scenario_versions

nodes.name: plays

nodes.name: events
nodes.summary: Append-only event log table recording each play step with sequence number, event_type enum, and JSONB delta for event-sourced replay.

nodes.name: reflections

nodes.name: upgrade
nodes.summary: Creates the versionstatus and eventtype enums and the five MVP tables with their indexes, foreign keys, and unique constraints.

nodes.name: downgrade
nodes.summary: Drops all MVP tables and the enum types in reverse dependency order to roll back the initial schema.

layers.name: Frontend / Learner UI
layers.description: Next.js App Router pages, scene renderer components, and the typed API client that deliver the interactive branching-scenario learner experience.

layers.name: API / HTTP Layer
layers.description: FastAPI application bootstrap, versioned admin/public routers, dependency injection, Pydantic request/response schemas, and core settings.

layers.name: Domain Services
layers.description: Business-logic services such as analytics aggregation and CSV export that orchestrate data between the API and persistence layers.

layers.name: Persistence / Data
layers.description: SQLAlchemy models, repositories, database session wiring, Alembic migrations, and the Postgres table definitions backing scenarios and plays.

layers.name: Scenario Engine
layers.description: Pure-Python state-machine runner that executes branching scenarios, including executors, state, models, and the scenario validator.

layers.name: Expression Parser
layers.description: Self-contained safe expression language: lexer, parser, AST nodes, and evaluator used to gate scenario branches.

layers.name: Infrastructure & Build
layers.description: Dockerfiles, docker-compose stack, build/tooling configs, dependency manifests, and operational seed scripts and fixtures.

layers.name: Documentation
layers.description: Project README, API and schema references, architecture decision records, and the canonical sample scenario.

layers.name: Tests
layers.description: Pytest suites for the API and packages, Jest component/page tests, Playwright end-to-end specs, and shared test fixtures.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start with the README to understand what the MVP delivers: interactive branching scenarios shared via links, where a FastAPI backend is the sole authority for execution and a Next.js frontend is a pure renderer. ADR-001 records why the team chose the Next.js + FastAPI + Postgres stack. Together these set the mental model the rest of the tour fills in code-by-code.

tour.title: Safe Expression Language (M1)
tour.description: The first TDD milestone builds a self-contained expression language used to gate scenario branches without ever calling eval(). Follow the pipeline bottom-up: tokens define the vocabulary, the lexer scans text into tokens, the recursive-descent parser builds an AST honoring operator precedence, and the evaluator walks that AST. safe_evaluate is the fail-closed entry point the engine later uses for conditional scenes.
tour.languageLesson: A recursive-descent parser encodes precedence as a ladder of methods (or/and/equality/comparison/unary/primary), each calling the next-tighter level. 'Fail-closed' means any parse or evaluation error resolves to a safe default (false) rather than raising into the caller.

tour.title: Why Custom Expressions
tour.description: Before moving on, read the decision record behind the parser you just toured. ADR-004 explains why a hand-written AST evaluator was mandated over Python's eval: security against arbitrary code execution, deterministic behavior, and a stable contract for scenario authors. This is the 'no eval, fail-closed' principle the README emphasizes, now justified.

tour.title: Scenario Engine (M2)
tour.description: Milestone two builds the pure-Python state machine that actually runs scenarios. models.py defines the scenario JSON contract as a Pydantic discriminated union of scene types (choice, auto_advance, conditional, end); state.py holds the mutable per-play working state and history; executors.py provides one pure function per scene type; and engine.py exposes the stateless ScenarioEngine that the API drives. The validator checks structural and referential integrity, reusing the expression parser from Step 2 to verify conditions.
tour.languageLesson: A Pydantic discriminated union uses a literal 'type' field to pick the right model variant during parsing, giving you type-safe handling of heterogeneous scene shapes. Executors operating on a defensive copy of state keep transitions pure, which is what makes deterministic replay possible.

tour.title: Event Sourcing & Go-Back
tour.description: The engine is stateless and deterministic for a reason: the backend rewinds by replaying immutable events, not by mutating state. ADR-002 captures this event-sourcing decision for the 'Go Back' feature, motivated by auditability, mutation-free rewind, and path analytics. Keep this in mind as the next steps introduce the events table and the replay logic in the play repository.

tour.title: Database Schema (M3)
tour.description: The persistence milestone begins with the Postgres schema. A single Alembic migration creates all five tables: scenarios and their versioned JSONB snapshots (scenario_versions), learner plays, the append-only events log that powers replay, and per-play reflections. ADR-003 explains why scenario_versions exists even in the MVP, and schema.md documents the tables for reference.
tour.languageLesson: Alembic migrations are ordered, versioned scripts with upgrade/downgrade pairs. The append-only events table embodies event sourcing: rows are never updated or deleted in normal flow, so the full play history can be replayed deterministically to reconstruct any prior state.

tour.title: ORM Models & Session
tour.description: These SQLAlchemy models map the schema from Step 6 into Python. base.py defines the declarative Base whose metadata aggregates every table (and feeds migrations), scenario.py and play.py define the ORM classes (play.py also covers Event and Reflection), and session.py wires the engine, session factory, and the get_db request dependency. scenario.py is the most depended-upon module in the backend, so it is worth reading closely.

tour.title: Repositories & Replay
tour.description: Repositories are the data-access layer between the API and the database. scenario_repo.py handles slug lookup, monotonic version numbering, and publish/archive transitions. play_repo.py is where event sourcing lives: it assigns sequence numbers to events, supports go-back by truncating the event log, and formats events for engine replay. This is the concrete implementation of the ADR-002 strategy from Step 5.

tour.title: API Bootstrap & Config
tour.description: Now the HTTP layer. main.py is the FastAPI entry point that wires CORS, mounts the admin and public v1 routers, and exposes a health check. config.py centralizes environment-driven settings (database URL, admin key, CORS origins), and deps.py provides the X-Admin-Key verification dependency that guards admin routes. These three tie together the persistence layer below and the routers above.

tour.title: Public Play API
tour.description: The learner-facing router drives a full playthrough: start, step, go-back, view, and reflection submission, all backed by the event-sourced engine and repositories from Steps 7-8. public.py is the highest fan-out module in the codebase, orchestrating engine, repos, and schemas. public.py (schemas) defines the Pydantic request/response shapes that become the API contract the frontend mirrors.

tour.title: Admin API & Services
tour.description: The admin side handles scenario authoring and reporting, guarded by the API-key dependency from Step 9. admin.py exposes scenario import, version creation/publishing, analytics, and CSV export, with matching schemas in admin.py (schemas). The domain services analytics.py and export.py do the heavy lifting: aggregating event/play data into reports and producing per-play CSV rows with reflection columns and derived scene paths.

tour.title: Frontend Renderer (M4)
tour.description: The UI milestone treats the frontend as a pure renderer and command dispatcher. types.ts mirrors the backend's public Pydantic schemas, and client.ts is the typed fetch wrapper for every endpoint. The play page drives the interactive loop (fetch state, dispatch step/back, redirect on completion), while SceneRenderer dispatches to the choice, continue, and end scene components, and ReflectionForm collects responses at the end.
tour.languageLesson: React Query (used by the client/pages) separates server state from UI state: queries fetch and cache play state, while mutations dispatch step/back commands and invalidate the cache. Keeping the frontend a thin renderer means all branching logic stays server-authoritative.

tour.title: Infrastructure & Seeding
tour.description: Finally, how it all runs. docker-compose.yml orchestrates Postgres, Adminer, the FastAPI backend (with migrations), and the Next.js frontend. The two Dockerfiles package each service, and seed.py imports/publishes a scenario fixture through the Admin API so a fresh database has playable content. SAMPLE_SCENARIO.json documents the JSON import contract authors must follow.
tour.languageLesson: Docker Compose's depends_on plus the migration-on-start pattern ensures the database is ready and schema-current before the API serves traffic. The web Dockerfile uses a multi-stage build (deps -> builder -> runner) to ship a minimal non-root production image.

tour.title: Test Strategy & TDD
tour.description: The project was built test-first, so the suites double as executable specs for each milestone. Package tests cover the expression parser and engine in isolation; the API integration tests exercise the public play lifecycle (start/step/back/reflection) against a transactional database fixture; and the Playwright smoke test walks a full playthrough through the real UI. Reading these confirms the behavior described throughout the tour.
